_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MBBS with relevant experience and training

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indications, planned line of treatment and advice for admission
Complete blood count (CBC); Urine albumin, sugar, microscopic examination

claim.label: Local swab for culture & sensitivity Report
Detailed Discharge Summary including care of the episiotomy site

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms & signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission available?
Detailed delivery notes (if available) including details of duration of labor, date of delivery and details of primary episiotomy repair?
Did clinical examination confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the clinical examination indicative of procedure?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: No recent history of delivery with episiotomy documented
Clinical examination findings not indicative of the need for secondary suturing of episiotomy
Detailed clinical notes are missing or do not clearly state indications, planned treatment, or advice for admission
Mandatory pre-authorization investigation reports (CBC, Urine albumin, sugar, microscopic examination) are missing or incomplete
Detailed operative/procedure notes are missing or incomplete
Discharge summary is missing or lacks follow-up advice for episiotomy site care

clinical_key_pointers: Secondary suturing of episiotomy is indicated in cases where there is dehiscence of the episiotomy.
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Infection
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Faulty repair of the primary episiotomy
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Anemia
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Vulva hematoma formation
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Poor personal hygiene
Possible reasons of dehiscence of episiotomy: Malnutrition
Common Presenting symptoms: Dysuria, with or without urinary retention
Secondary suturing is done after ensuring: Debridement of necrotic tissue
Secondary suturing is done after ensuring: Daily care of the wound till there are no signs of infection
Secondary suturing is done after ensuring: Healthy pink granulation tissue at the episiotomy site
Secondary suturing is done after ensuring: Adequate analgesia/anaesthesia
contraindications: Presence of slough / unhealthy exudate / pus discharge at the episiotomy site
